must_detect.matched_text:
Arunchai Logistics Public Company Limited (SSE: ARL) — Cyber/Operational Incident Report (Non-public) — 19/05/2569, 21:40 ICT
Summary: On 18–19 May 2569, ransomware encryption disrupted WMS and invoicing at Bangkok Distribution Hub; estimated downtime 11 hours; preliminary impact ~3–5% of consolidated Q2/2569 revenue and potential margin effect; issuer-assessed materiality under SLA pending.
Public-status: This constitutes inside information not yet disseminated via SSENews; per the Exchange Disclosure Manual, we intend to lodge an immediate report within T+1 business day or earlier if the threshold is met; internal trading blackout remains in force through the quiet period until Q2 results and MD&A are released.
Selective disclosure: Information is shared only with lenders and rating advisors under NDA as permitted selective disclosure (no broad release), including Mekhong Bank PCL and Naga Ratings Co., Ltd., solely to support covenant discussions.
Personal data exposure (PDPA context): 17 employee records may have been accessed; affected include Khun Piriya Janthana (EID E-7 4 2 1 1; email: piriya.j@arl-logi.co.th); no national ID scans or biometric templates were found in exfiltration logs.
Systems and scope: Attack vector tied to third-party courier API; asset ID HUB-03-WIN-CAGE; sample filenames INV-2 5 6 9 0 1 4.pdf and route_—_1A.csv; backup vault remained intact; customer bank a/c numbers were not stored in the impacted segment.
Countermeasures: Isolation completed; decryption in progress without paying ransom; vendor TigerShield Cyber Defense Co., Ltd. retained under MSA TSCD-2024-017; cross-border processing at LotusGate Cloud Pte. Ltd. (ap-sea-1) under DPA Addendum §4 was suspended.
Legal/regulatory: We assess obligations under the Securities and Listings Code B.E. 2599, PDPA Sections relating to breach notification, and the Exchange’s immediate disclosure rules; a draft report to the Commission will be prepared for pre-clearance.
Public references for context: As already announced in ARL’s SSENews No. ARL 006/2569 dated 01/05/2569 re “Solar Rooftop Pilot” and in Form 56-1X One Review FY2568 filed on 31/03/2569, our ESG roadmap and hub expansion are public and unaffected by this incident.
Financing and covenants: Current preliminary estimate does not trigger a material adverse change clause, and no drawstop is anticipated; working capital facilities remain undrawn; this sentence is generic policy text and is not price-sensitive by itself.
Deal/ops note: This incident does not alter the publicly disclosed logistics tariffs; unrelated third-party press releases about barcode scanner models and toner schedules have no bearing here.
Insider controls: The blackout window is extended to directors, executives, and Section 60 persons of the issuer and subsidiaries; any selective communications must use the template “ARL-IR-Note-INC/2569-05A” and be tracked in the log.
Tracking and URLs: Incident case https://ir.arl-pcl.co.th/sse?doc=ARL-IR-Note-INC%2F2569%2F05A&v=1 and vault ref VLT-9x-—-BKK; screenshot hash b3d1:9c0e::7f:…; helpline: incident@arl-pcl.co.th.
Next steps and timing: We expect service normalization by 20/05/2569 06:00 ICT; if margin impact exceeds the internal 3% threshold, we will disclose sooner via SSENews and include details in Q2/2569 financial statements and MD&A.
Contact: Ms. Warunee Phromjit, Company Secretary, co.sec@arl-pcl.co.th, +66-2-6x4-45x8; for IR: Khun Chanin Rattapong, ir@arl-pcl.co.th; do not post externally; project
